title: Smoked Pepper Italian Potato Gnocchi
description: A hearty, crispy Italian dish featuring smoky flavors and wild garlic leaves. Perfect for a quick weeknight dinner, this recipe is kosher-friendly and customizable to various dietary needs.

ingredients.name: Wild garlic leaves
ingredients.notes: Also known as ramps, find them at farmers' markets or substitute with scallions.

ingredients.name: Smoked red pepper flakes
ingredients.notes: Alternatively, use regular red pepper flakes.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince or crush for best flavor.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use kosher salt for best flavor.

instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
Add the potatoes and cook until they're tender when pierced with a fork, about 15-20 minutes.
Drain the potatoes and let them cool slightly.
Transfer the potatoes to a mixing bowl and mash until they're smooth.
Add the egg, flour, smoked mozzarella, Parmesan cheese, smoked red pepper flakes, garlic, and salt. Mix well until a dough forms.
Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead for 5-7 minutes, until the dough is smooth and elastic.
Roll the dough into a long rope and cut it into 1-inch pieces to form the gnocchi.
Bring the salted water back to a boil and cook the gnocchi for 2-3 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
Remove the gnocchi with a slotted spoon and transfer them to a plate.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the wild garlic leaves and cook until they're wilted, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the cooked gnocchi to the skillet and toss with the wild garlic and oil.
Season with salt to taste and serve hot, garnished with parsley if desired.
